seo.title: Ying Yang | Beihang University
seo.description: Ying Yang — Professor at the School of Physics, Beihang University. Research in fluorescence microscopy and multiplex imaging.

hero.title: YANG LAB
hero.subtitle:
We are an interdisciplinary research group at the interface of physics, chemistry, and the life sciences. We develop innovative fluorescence imaging technologies to address key questions in biology. By integrating electrochemical fluorescence control with advanced super-resolution microscopy, we pioneer electrically controlled fluorescence microscopy as a new imaging framework to visualize and quantify molecular interactions at the nanoscale, enable large-field-of-view imaging, and observe multiple cellular structures simultaneously.

Our research focuses on the precise control of fluorescence emission across space and time using chemical and physical approaches. We aim to push the boundaries of fluorescence microscopy in resolution, imaging depth, multiplexing capability, and quantitative analysis.
hero.image_alt: Conceptual diagram showing applied potential tuning fluorophore emission from low to high fluorescence intensity

about.heading: Curiosity, made visible.
about.lead: My research explores new ways to observe, control, and understand fluorescence at fine spatial and temporal scales.
biography_en: Ying Yang is a Professor in the School of Physics at Beihang University. She received her PhD in Chemistry from UNSW Sydney and subsequently continued her postdoctoral research there. Her research spans physical chemistry, biosensing, advanced fluorescence microscopy, fundamental electrochemistry, interfacial and surface chemistry, and super-resolution optical imaging.

Her current work focuses on the electrochemical control of fluorophore redox states and its application to microscopy, with an emphasis on electrically controlled fluorescence methods for multicolor and super-resolution biological imaging. She has published 12 first-author papers in journals including Nature Photonics, PNAS, Nature Communications, Accounts of Chemical Research, and Angewandte Chemie International Edition.

team.heading: People & collaboration
team.intro: We welcome curious minds interested in optical imaging, fluorescence microscopy, photo-physics.
